summary.what: Render timbers errors as a single plain line when stderr is not a TTY
summary.why: An agent misread a blocked commit as success: the pre-commit gate rejected it, but 'git commit 2>&1 | tail -1' and rtk compression showed a blank line. Root cause isn't the gate — it's fang. fang.Execute wraps stderr in a colorprofile.Writer before calling the error handler, so DefaultErrorHandler's 'w.(term.File)' non-TTY guard never fires and the padded ERROR box (which ends in a blank line) renders even into a pipe; tail/compressors then crop the failure to nothing. Chose a custom fang error handler that checks the real os.Stderr over the narrower alternative of os.Exit-in-the-gate: the handler is testable (no os.Exit killing the test process), and it fixes EVERY piped timbers error, not just the gate. Kept fang's styled box for interactive TTYs.
summary.how: Added newErrorHandler(stderrIsTTY) returning a fang.ErrorHandler: TTY delegates to fang.DefaultErrorHandler, non-TTY prints err.Error() as one plain line. Wired via fang.WithErrorHandler(newErrorHandler(output.IsTTY(os.Stderr))) in main. Routed the pre-commit gate's [timbers] advice block to stderr (diagnostic; keeps stdout clean) and made the returned gate error self-contained (names tool, cause, fix, bypass) so the one line that survives tail -1 is actionable. Regression test asserts the non-TTY single-line contract.

notes: os.Exit-in-gate alternative rejected: untestable + only fixes the gate. Independent review clean across all 6 areas. Pre-existing nit (useColor checks stdout while the gate printer now writes stderr) left out of scope — the block applies no styling so it's moot.
